metadata.name: DeveloperMenu
metadata.description: Guides > Developer menu. How to wrap an iOS (SwiftJsonUI) or Android (KotlinJsonUI) app root in DeveloperMenuContainer so double-tap opens a screen switcher and long-press toggles Dynamic Mode (hot reload). Seven sections + TOC + next-reads. Web (ReactJsonUI) has no equivalent — the lead calls this out. Docs site itself is Web, so platforms = ["web"]; the article TOPIC is iOS/Android but the RENDERING target is Web like every other guide.

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_navigation -> /guides/navigation, next_writing_layouts -> /guides/writing-layouts) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the guides_developer_menu_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped URLs enumerated in transitions: /, /guides/navigation, /guides/writing-layouts.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /guides/navigation and /guides/writing-layouts. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog.

relatedFiles.description: Layout source of truth (hand-edited, distributed to jsonui-doc-web/src/Layouts/guides/developer-menu.json by jui build).

notes: Seventh live entry under the Guides tab. Follows the same template as the other six (writing-your-first-spec, writing-layouts, navigation, testing, localization, custom-components).

Seven sections: (1) What it is — DEBUG-only container, double-tap opens screen selector, long-press toggles Dynamic Mode, Release = passthrough, iOS + Android only; (2) Conform your Screen enum to DeveloperScreen — iOS protocol requires `name: String`, Android interface requires `displayName: String`; (3) iOS — wrap WindowGroup body in DeveloperMenuContainer with @State/$Binding and switch-based content closure; (4) Android — wrap setContent in DeveloperMenuContainer, derive currentScreen from NavController backstack, route onScreenChange through navController.navigate; (5) Dynamic Mode toggle — long-press flips ViewSwitcher (iOS) / DynamicModeManager (Android); (6) Release builds and the enabled flag — enabled = BuildConfig.DEBUG pattern; (7) Pitfalls — exhaustive switch, dual-state trap on Android, don't forget enabled flag, gesture conflicts with content long-press, empty displayName.

Running exemplars: whisky-find-agent iOS (whisky_find_agentApp.swift wraps the SwiftUI App root) and whisky-find-agent Android (MainActivity.kt wraps setContent around the NavHost). CodeBlocks use language: 'swift' and 'kotlin' — both are in the Shiki preloaded list.

Web (ReactJsonUI) has no DeveloperMenuContainer equivalent. The lead states this explicitly, and Sidebar / GuidesIndex display [iOS] [Android] platform pills (Phase 0 infrastructure added alongside this guide) so readers see the platform scope before opening the page.
